SCHOOL::Beginning Latin III::Lesson 21. Review first and second declension adjectives (lesson XX). Read the twenty-first lesson. Oral drill: decline the adjective"magnus, a, um" and the noun "servus, i." Then put them together, remembering that "servus" is masculine. Do the Day 1 exercises.Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisservus, servi, servo, servum, servo, servi, servorum, servis, servos, servismagnus servus, magni servi, magno servo, magnum servum, magno servo, magni servi, magnorum servorum, magnis servis,magnos servos, magnis servis

SCHOOL::Beginning Latin III::Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and "to be" verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective "magnus, a, um" and the noun "dux, ducis." Then put them together, remembering that "dux" is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibus

SCHOOL::Religion 8::Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the four cardinal virtues. Discuss Chapter 21 of Our Life in the Church using "Words to Know"and the discussion questions in Chapter 21 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercisescould be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 21 ofthe activity book as a guide.) These can be done orally. The discussion will prepare you for the summaries in the next two days.

SCHOOL::Beginning Latin III::Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and "to be" verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective "magnus, a, um" and the noun "dux, ducis." Then put them together, remembering that "dux" is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibus
SCHOOL::English 8::Do the Verb Review today, pages 108-110. (T.E. pages 206-211.)

SCHOOL::History & Geog 7::Locate a good map of the Mediterranean Area. Trace (or draw freehand) the outline of Italy, including the Mediterranean region. Themaps on pp. 14-19 of Historical Atlas of the World by Rand McNally are all good maps for this. Photocopy it 6 times. Also, read TheOld World and America pp. 62-73.

SCHOOL::Religion 7::Discuss the questions for Chapter 23. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What device did Paul use to deflect interest from himself?2. How did Paul escape from the plot of the Jews?3. Where was Paul sent?4. Did the governor hear Paul's case immediately?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.
